sourceMovie.title: Migration
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Migration works as a family movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The family and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fantastic Mr. Fox
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Migration clicked for you, Fantastic Mr. Fox (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Migration clicked for you, Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. faq.question: What should I watch after Migration?
faq.answer: Try Fantastic Mr. Fox, Wallace & Gromit: The Curse of the Were-Rabbit, Storks—each shares DNA with Migration while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Migration?
faq.answer: Migration (2023) is best described as Family, Comedy, Adventure, Animation. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Migration worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this family lane.
